title: ranunculus auricomus f. cervicornis
intro: Ranunculus auricomus f. cervicornis, also known as the golden buttercup or lesser meadow rue, is a perennial plant species within the Ranunculaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and gout. The health benefits of Ranunculus auricomus f. cervicornis include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and easing symptoms of arthritis and other joint-related conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties, although caution is advised due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
